input.headline: Thousands of people 'trapped' on M7 every day, Dáil hears

raw.headline: Thousands of people 'trapped' on M7 every day, Dáil hears

winner: I'd like to report that we've now officially classified "thousands of people being stuck on the M7" as a mild inconvenience, rather than the emergency situation our ancestors dealt with—back when they actually had to walk somewhere.

candidates.text: Why did everyone get stuck on the M7? Because they thought it was a *toll* road with no *exit* strategy!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'toll road' and 'exit strategy' are clever and specific to the headline, with good surprise factor and natural language."}

candidates.text: Why did the M7 motorway get a restraining order? Because it kept trapping thousands of people—even when they weren’t breaking any laws!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline with a clever twist involving a restraining order."}

candidates.text: Why did the motorist bring a ladder to the M7? He wanted to reach new heights in gridlock!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'gridlock' and 'reach new heights' are clever and specific to the context of traffic on the M7."}

candidates.text: I'd like to report that we've now officially classified "thousands of people being stuck on the M7" as a mild inconvenience, rather than the emergency situation our ancestors dealt with—back when they actually had to walk somewhere.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising historical comparison adds humor; concise and specific with a touch of irony."}

candidates.text: Thousands of people are trapped on the M7 every day," the politician announced. "But don't worry, we've got a solution—just add more traffic lights.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the context of traffic on the M7, but the solution ('add more traffic lights') is not particularly funny or clever, and feels slightly generic."}
